content.thinking:
I can see:
1. The main `ingress-nginx-controller` ConfigMap already has OpenTelemetry enabled with:
   - `enable-opentelemetry: "true"`
   - `opentelemetry-collector-host: otel-collector.otel.svc`
   - `opentelemetry-collector-port: "4317"`
   - `otel-sample-rate: "1.0"`
   - `log-format-upstream` with otel_trace_id and otel_span_id

But wait, looking at the annotations, it shows the `last-applied-configuration` was `{"apiVersion":"v1","data":null,...}` which means the data was null and was patched separately. 

The ConfigMap data got merged in somehow. Looking at the resource versions, the main ConfigMap is at 1710036 while the extra one was created at 1710015, so the main one has the current settings. I should check if the controller deployment is actually using the right ConfigMap, look at the controller logs for any reload errors, and verify whether the nginx configuration is working properly given the task warning about potential invalid patches.

content.text: The main ConfigMap already has OpenTelemetry settings. Let me check if the controller is running correctly and if there are reload errors.

content.thinking:
So there are reload errors happening. The last reload at 02:53:41 was successful though. The errors are at 02:53:30, 02:53:34, and 02:53:37. The last reload at 02:53:41 succeeded.

The adversary note says the ConfigMap may have been patched with invalid data. Let me check what the current state of the ConfigMap looks like more carefully, and also check if there's a `otel-alt` ingress and what's going on with it.

Let me also look at the full nginx config to understand what's loaded.

content.text: There were reload errors but the last reload succeeded. Let me check the current nginx configuration to verify OTel settings are properly loaded.

input.command: kubectl -n ingress-nginx exec deploy/ingress-nginx-controller -- cat /etc/nginx/nginx.conf 2>/dev/null | grep -E "opentelemetry|otel|trace|span|log_format" | head -30

content.thinking:
The nginx config already has the OTel settings and log format correctly configured. The log format includes `otel_trace_id=$opentelemetry_trace_id otel_span_id=$opentelemetry_span_id`.

There's an `otel-echo` ingress with host `otel.example.com` and an `otel-alt` ingress with host `otel-alt.example.com`.

Let me check the OTel TOML config to see if sampling is set to 100%.

content.content:
exporter = "otlp"
processor = "batch"

[exporters.otlp]
# Alternatively the OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT environment variable can also be used.
host = "otel-collector.otel.svc"
port = 4317

[processors.batch]
max_queue_size = 2048
schedule_delay_millis = 5000
max_export_batch_size = 512

[service]
name = "nginx"

[sampler]
name = "AlwaysOn"
ratio = 1.0
parent_based = false

content.thinking:
The OTel configuration already has `AlwaysOn` sampler with ratio 1.0 (100% sampling). The log format already includes the trace and span IDs. 

Now let me verify the demo setup and run the test request to see if we get trace IDs in the logs.

content.text: The OTel config looks correct with `AlwaysOn` sampler at 100% ratio. Let me run the test request and check the logs.

input.command: kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- curl -sS -H "Host: otel.example.com" http://ingress-nginx-controller.ingress-nginx.svc/otel-check 2>&1
input.description: Send test request via curl-test pod

input.command: kubectl -n ingress-nginx logs deploy/ingress-nginx-controller --tail=20 2>&1 | grep -E "otel_check|otel_trace_id|otel_span_id" | head -10
